hallucinations.description: Multiple references cite nonexistent 2025 arXiv papers (e.g., CosmoCore arXiv:2510.18895, Sol-Ver arXiv:2502.14948, Self-Challenging arXiv:2506.01716) that do not exist in real academic literature.
hallucinations.evidence: References section: [1] Ravindran, S. K. (2025). CosmoCore... arXiv:2510.18895; [8] Lin et al. (2025). Learning to Solve and Verify... arXiv:2502.14948; [9] Zhou et al. (2025). Self-Challenging... arXiv:2506.01716.

hallucinations.description: The paper reports iterative improvement via ExePlay alignment (1.56% to 2.34% pass rate) and presents DPO training results, but the provided source code only generates preference pairs without performing any model updates or DPO optimization.
hallucinations.evidence: Section 5.2 and Algorithm 1 claim model optimization via L_ExePlay; limitations section admits 'No Full DPO Training' and 'actual model weights were not updated'; run_exeplay_experiment() collects pairs but never calls training/optimization.

hallucinations.description: The methodology section describes a full self-play loop with contrastive alignment and model updates, but the implementation and experiments only perform generation and pair construction without any actual alignment training.
hallucinations.evidence: Section 3.4 and 3.5 detail Weighted DPO loss and 'Update model' step; code in run_experiment.py and exeplay_framework.py shows no model fine-tuning occurs across iterations.

overall_assessment: Hallucinations were found: nonexistent citations, faked experimental results from unperformed DPO training, and hallucinated methodology describing training that never occurred. Severity is high due to fabricated references and misleading performance claims.
